questions.question: Explain the difference between the Adapter and Decorator patterns with examples.
expectedPoints: Adapter changes interface, Decorator adds functionality
Adapter makes incompatible interfaces work together
Decorator dynamically adds responsibilities without subclassing
Decorator example: adding features to UI components

questions.question: Compare and contrast three structural design patterns, discussing their applications, advantages, and limitations.
expectedPoints: Adapter: interface conversion, legacy integration, but can create complex code
Decorator: dynamic functionality addition, flexible but can result in many small objects
Composite: tree structure handling, uniform treatment but can make design overly general
Proxy: access control, security benefits but adds indirection
When to choose one pattern over another
